title: Dominium Capability Surface Taxonomy
repo_status: post-Ω/Ξ/Π and post-P-0..P-4 and post-0-0 and post-Λ-0 and post-Λ-1

core_definition.summary: A capability surface is a lawful, machine-readable interaction surface exposed over domain-grounded substrate and affordances. It describes who may attempt what, under which conditions, and through which lawful Process routes, without becoming truth, observation, or UI.

capability_surface_classes.summary: Direct embodied interaction with tools, bodies, materials, and nearby environmental features.
Position-bound or slot-bound access surfaces such as entry, docking, mounting, seating, or operating stations.
Control and routing surfaces for movement, piloting, dispatch, loading, and transport coordination.
Input, output, and control surfaces over machines, instruments, and process hosts.
Access, switching, linking, or traversal surfaces over graph, route, and signal structures.
Authority, policy, jurisdiction, assignment, governance, and coordination surfaces over organizations and institutions.
Abstract planning, delegation, reservation, and command surfaces over lawful substrate.
Inspection, measurement, audit, probe, and diagnostic surfaces for lawful observation actions.
Service, repair, calibration, upkeep, and restoration surfaces.
Machine-readable orchestration, protocol, and automation surfaces over lawful capabilities.
Adapter surfaces that map lawful capability surfaces into specific interface modes without changing capability meaning.

actor_classes.summary: A player acting through embodied, local, diegetic, or otherwise physically constrained pathways.
lawful_access_notes: Embodied access may require proximity, occupancy, tooling, or bodily compatibility.
Embodied access is not the only valid access form, but it remains law-governed.

actor_classes.summary: A non-player embodied actor acting through lawful local pathways.
lawful_access_notes: NPC access remains grounded in the same substrate and does not receive silent special powers.

actor_classes.summary: A planning actor using abstract, command, reservation, or delegation surfaces.
lawful_access_notes: Abstract planning must not grant impossible powers absent from lawful substrate.
Planning surfaces still depend on authority, scope, and host validity.

actor_classes.summary: An AI agent operating through governed, machine-readable capability surfaces.
lawful_access_notes: AI interaction remains capability- and authority-bound.
AI access must not bypass Law, refusal, or epistemic limits.

actor_classes.summary: An organizational or institutional actor acting through formal authority structures.
lawful_access_notes: Institutional action requires recognized role, jurisdiction, or formal authority.

actor_classes.summary: A machine or automation system using explicit integration and automation surfaces.
lawful_access_notes: Automation is not implicit permission. It must remain verified, compatible, and authority-bound.

actor_classes.summary: A remote operator acting through lawful remote or mediated surfaces.
lawful_access_notes: Remote access changes path, not ontology.
Remote access must not erase physical or institutional limits that still matter.

actor_classes.summary: A future interface or adapter layer that maps onto existing lawful capability surfaces.
lawful_access_notes: Adapters may translate surfaces into new modalities but may not redefine capability meaning.

relationship_notes.capability_surface_vs_affordance: Affordances are domain-level actionable relationships. Capability surfaces expose those affordances as explicit interaction surfaces for actor classes and interface paths.
relationship_notes.capability_surface_vs_recognition: Recognitions identify or classify structure. Capability surfaces may attach to recognized structures but do not replace recognition or substrate meaning.
relationship_notes.capability_surface_vs_observation_bindin: Observation bindings expose what can be perceived or inspected. Capability surfaces expose what can be attempted. Detectability and usability are distinct.
relationship_notes.capability_surface_vs_ui_binding: UI and control bindings are presentation and input mappings over capability surfaces. They are not the capability surfaces themselves.
relationship_notes.capability_surface_vs_truth: Capability surfaces do not create or mutate truth by fiat. They route lawful attempts into Processes under Law and AuthorityContext.

extensibility_rules: Later domains may specialize capability-surface classes, but only as explicit refinements of the constitutional classes listed here.
Later domains must link capability surfaces back to domain contracts, affordances, host structures, and lawful Process families.
New actor classes require explicit artifact updates rather than ad hoc local invention.
Interface adapters may extend presentation and invocation pathways, but must not widen capability meaning silently.
linkage_expectations: Each instantiated capability surface must reference at least one domain contract.
Each instantiated capability surface should reference linked affordances or host structures explicitly.
AuthorityContext remains the canonical authority envelope for admission and observation scope.
Observation channels and detectability rules remain distinct from capability existence and authorization.
Interface adapters map to existing lawful surfaces instead of becoming new ontology.

later_lambda: Λ-3 representation ladders can refine how capability surfaces project across representations.
Λ-4 formalization chain can attach proof and substitution criteria to capability surfaces.
Λ-5 can map player desire and refusal onto lawful capability classes.
Λ-6 can bridge domain capability surfaces without collapsing ownership.
sigma: Σ can govern human, agent, tool, and MCP interaction against one shared capability vocabulary.
phi: Φ can map input, runtime service, planner, automation, and interface abstractions onto lawful capability surfaces instead of ad hoc tags.
